params.name: PRIM_NAME
params.comment: Sets the prim's name.

params.name: PRIM_DESC
params.comment: Sets the prim's description.

params.name: PRIM_SLICE
params.comment: Sets the prim's slice (a shape attribute).

params.name: PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_TYPE
params.comment: Sets the prim's physics shape type.

params.name: PRIM_MATERIAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's material.

params.name: PRIM_PHYSICS
params.comment: Sets the object's physics status.

params.name: PRIM_TEMP_ON_REZ
params.comment: Sets the object's temporary attribute.

params.name: PRIM_PHANTOM
params.comment: Sets the object's phantom status.

params.name: PRIM_POSITION
params.comment: Sets the prim's position.

params.name: PRIM_POS_LOCAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's local position.

params.name: PRIM_ROTATION
params.comment: Sets the prim's global rotation.

params.name: PRIM_ROT_LOCAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's local rotation.

params.name: PRIM_SIZE
params.comment: Sets the prim's size.

params.name: PRIM_TEXTURE
params.comment: Sets the prim's texture attributes.

params.name: PRIM_RENDER_MATERIAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's render_material. Setting this param will also clear most PRIM_GLTF_* properties on the face, with the exceptions of repeats, offsets, and rotation_in_radians

params.name: PRIM_TEXT
params.comment: Sets the prim's floating text.

params.name: PRIM_COLOR
params.comment: Sets the face's color.

params.name: PRIM_BUMP_SHINY
params.comment: Sets the face's shiny & bump.

params.name: PRIM_POINT_LIGHT
params.comment: Sets the prim as a point light.

params.name: PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE
params.comment: Sets the prim as a reflection probe.

params.name: PRIM_FULLBRIGHT
params.comment: Sets the face's full bright flag.

params.name: PRIM_FLEXIBLE
params.comment: Sets the prim as flexible.

params.name: PRIM_TEXGEN
params.comment: Sets the face's texture mode.

params.name: PRIM_GLOW
params.comment: Sets the face's glow attribute.

params.name: PRIM_OMEGA
params.comment: Sets the prim's spin to the specified axis and rate.

params.name: PRIM_NORMAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's normal map attributes.

params.name: PRIM_SPECULAR
params.comment: Sets the prim's specular map attributes.

params.name: PRIM_ALPHA_MODE
params.comment: Sets the prim's diffuse texture alpha rendering mode attributes.

params.name: PRIM_LINK_TARGET
params.comment: Sets the next linknumber to use in the linkset.

params.name: PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS
params.comment: Sets the prim's cast shadow attribute. (DEPRECATED)

params.name: PRIM_ALLOW_UNSIT
params.comment: Avatars are allowed to manually stand up when seated on this prim. Only valid for prims in a valid experience.

params.name: PRIM_SCRIPTED_SIT_ONLY
params.comment: Avatars are not permitted to manually sit on this prim.

params.name: PRIM_SIT_TARGET
params.comment: The sit target, if any defined for this prim.

params.name: PRIM_PROJECTOR
params.comment: Light projector settings for this prim.

params.name: PRIM_CLICK_ACTION
params.comment: Click action for this prim

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR
params.comment: Sets the prim's GLTF Material Base Color map attributes. This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF. gltf_alpha_mode Flags V Description PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_OPAQUE 0 Ignore the alpha value and render the material as opaque. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND 1 Render the material with transparency determined by the alpha value. Blending is done in linear color space. As is the case for Blinn-Phong as well, this mode suffers from depth sorting and performance issues. Use alpha mask instead when possible. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_MASK 2 Render the material as fully opaque where the alpha value is greater than the alpha cutoff, and otherwise render the material as fully transparent.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_NORMAL
params.comment: Sets the prim's GLTF Material Normal map attributes. This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_METALLIC_ROUGHNESS
params.comment: Sets the prim's GLTF ORM map attributes (Occlusion, Roughness, Metallic). This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_EMISSIVE
params.comment: Sets the prim's GLTF Material Emissive map attributes. This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_SIT_FLAGS
params.comment: Sets the flags on the prim's sit target Flag Description SIT_FLAG_SIT_TARGET 0x1 Read-only flag to indicate whether the link has a sit target. Use llSitTarget, llLinkSitTarget, or PRIM_SIT_TARGET to disable or enable this flag. Use llGetLinkSitFlags, or llGetLinkPrimitiveParams with PRIM_SIT_FLAGS to read this flag. SIT_FLAG_ALLOW_UNSIT 0x2 Allow an avatar to manually unsit from a sit target. Only applies to agents who had been seated via an LSL script. See llSitOnLink. SIT_FLAG_SCRIPTED_ONLY 0x4 Only allow scripted sits on this sit target. SIT_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE 0x10 Disable the avatar's collision volume when they are seated on this sit target. SIT_FLAG_NO_DAMAGE 0x20 Do not distribute damage to agents sitting on this sit target.

params.name: PRIM_DAMAGE
params.comment: Sets the damage and damage type delivered by a prim on collision.

params.name: PRIM_HEALTH
params.comment: Sets the health value for this prim.

params.name: PRIM_NAME
params.comment: Name: llGetObjectName

params.name: PRIM_DESC
params.comment: Description: llGetObjectDesc

params.name: PRIM_TYPE
params.comment: Gets the prim shape. [Would you like to know more?][Hide]

params.name: PRIM_SLICE
params.comment: Gets the prim's slice (a shape attribute).

params.name: PRIM_PHYSICS_SHAPE_TYPE
params.comment: Gets the prim's physics shape type.

params.name: PRIM_MATERIAL
params.comment: Gets the prim's material. The material determines the default collision sound, sprite, friction coefficient and restitution coefficient.

params.name: PRIM_PHYSICS
params.comment: Physics status llGetStatus

params.name: PRIM_TEMP_ON_REZ
params.comment: Temporary attribute

params.name: PRIM_PHANTOM
params.comment: Phantom status llGetStatus

params.name: PRIM_POSITION
params.comment: Position, llGetPos

params.name: PRIM_POS_LOCAL
params.comment: Local position, llGetLocalPos

params.name: PRIM_ROTATION
params.comment: Global rotation, llGetRot

params.name: PRIM_ROT_LOCAL
params.comment: Local rotation, llGetLocalRot

params.name: PRIM_SIZE
params.comment: Size, llGetScale

params.name: PRIM_TEXTURE
params.comment: Texture: llGetTexture Repeats: llGetTextureScale Offset: llGetTextureOffset Rotation: llGetTextureRot

params.name: PRIM_RENDER_MATERIAL
params.comment: Material: llGetRenderMaterial

params.name: PRIM_TEXT
params.comment: Floating Text: NA

params.name: PRIM_COLOR
params.comment: Alpha: llGetAlpha Color: llGetColor

params.name: PRIM_POINT_LIGHT
params.comment: linear_color param accepts color in Linear space - use llsRGB2Linear to convert regular LSL color into Linear space.

params.name: PRIM_REFLECTION_PROBE
params.comment: Gets the prim's reflection probe parameters.

params.name: PRIM_OMEGA
params.comment: llTargetOmega

params.name: PRIM_LINK_TARGET
params.comment: Multiple llGetLinkPrimitiveParams calls.

params.name: PRIM_CAST_SHADOWS
params.comment: DEPRECATED: Shadow casting for the primitive

params.name: PRIM_SIT_TARGET
params.comment: Sit target, llSitTarget. The position can be ZERO_VECTOR.

params.name: PRIM_PROJECTOR
params.comment: Light projector settings, the texture may be NULL_KEY. (Write only, for now. See here)

params.name: PRIM_CLICK_ACTION
params.comment: sets the default action to take when a user clicks on this prim. Flag Description Cursor CLICK_ACTION_NONE 0 Performs the default action: when the prim is touched, touch events are triggered CLICK_ACTION_TOUCH 0 When the prim is touched, touch events are triggered CLICK_ACTION_SIT 1 When the prim is touched, the avatar sits upon it CLICK_ACTION_BUY 2 When the prim is touched, the buy dialog is opened CLICK_ACTION_PAY 3 When the prim is touched, the pay dialog is opened CLICK_ACTION_OPEN 4 When the prim is touched, the object inventory dialog is opened CLICK_ACTION_PLAY 5 Play or pause parcel media on touch CLICK_ACTION_OPEN_MEDIA 6 Play parcel media on touch, no pause CLICK_ACTION_ZOOM 7 Zoom the avatar camera on this object (Viewer 2) CLICK_ACTION_DISABLED 8 No click action. No touches detected or passed. CLICK_ACTION_IGNORE 9 Clicks go through the object to whatever is behind it. No touches detected.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_BASE_COLOR
params.comment: linear_color param accepts color in Linear space - use llsRGB2Linear to convert regular LSL color into Linear space. This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF. gltf_alpha_mode Flags V Description PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_OPAQUE 0 Ignore the alpha value and render the material as opaque. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND 1 Render the material with transparency determined by the alpha value. Blending is done in linear color space. As is the case for Blinn-Phong as well, this mode suffers from depth sorting and performance issues. Use alpha mask instead when possible. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_MASK 2 Render the material as fully opaque where the alpha value is greater than the alpha cutoff, and otherwise render the material as fully transparent.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_NORMAL
params.comment: This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_METALLIC_ROUGHNESS
params.comment: This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_GLTF_EMISSIVE
params.comment: emissive_tint param accepts color in Linear space - use llsRGB2Linear to convert regular LSL color into Linear space. This parameter's arguments are GLTF overrides. ⚠️ Warning: Setting an argument to the empty string ("") will clear the respective override. GLTF texture transforms are always overrides, so setting them to the empty string ("") will clear them. See this example for a workaround. The SL team is open to feedback on LSL improvements for GLTF.

params.name: PRIM_SIT_FLAGS
params.comment: Gets the sit flags currently set on this prim. Flag Description SIT_FLAG_SIT_TARGET 0x1 Read-only flag to indicate whether the link has a sit target. Use llSitTarget, llLinkSitTarget, or PRIM_SIT_TARGET to disable or enable this flag. Use llGetLinkSitFlags, or llGetLinkPrimitiveParams with PRIM_SIT_FLAGS to read this flag. SIT_FLAG_ALLOW_UNSIT 0x2 Allow an avatar to manually unsit from a sit target. Only applies to agents who had been seated via an LSL script. See llSitOnLink. SIT_FLAG_SCRIPTED_ONLY 0x4 Only allow scripted sits on this sit target. SIT_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE 0x10 Disable the avatar's collision volume when they are seated on this sit target. SIT_FLAG_NO_DAMAGE 0x20 Do not distribute damage to agents sitting on this sit target.

params.name: PRIM_DAMAGE
params.comment: Gets the damage and damage type delivered by a prim on collision.

params.name: PRIM_HEALTH
params.comment: Gets the health of a prim

params.name: HTTP_METHOD
params.comment: "GET", "POST", "PUT" and "DELETE"

params.name: HTTP_MIMETYPE
params.comment: text/* MIME types should specify a charset. To emulate HTML forms use application/x-www-form-urlencoded. This allows you to set the body to a properly escaped (llEscapeURL) sequence of <name,value> pairs in the form var=value&var2=value2 and have them automatically parsed by web frameworks. MIME types must be specified in the format: type/subtype[;option=value] Some valid examples are "text/html" "text/plain;charset=utf-8" "application/xhtml+xml" "application/json" "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" "application/rss+xml" "multipart/mixed; boundary="---1234567890---""

params.name: HTTP_BODY_MAXLENGTH
params.comment: Sets the maximum (UTF-8 encoded) byte length of the HTTP response body. The maximum that can be set depends upon which VM is used. Mono Max: 16384 LSO Max: 4096 ⚠️ Warning: Applies to the Outgoing pipeline only (HTTP calls invoked by llHTTPRequest,and responses from http_response). Tip: When you only need to request a small amount of data from a remote source, consider using the Content-Range header instead.

params.name: HTTP_VERIFY_CERT
params.comment: If TRUE, the server SSL certificate must be verifiable using one of the standard certificate authorities[1] when making HTTPS requests. If FALSE, any server SSL certificate will be accepted.

params.name: HTTP_VERBOSE_THROTTLE
params.comment: If TRUE, shout error messages to DEBUG_CHANNEL if the outgoing request rate exceeds the server limit. If FALSE, the error messages are suppressed (llHTTPRequest will still return NULL_KEY).

params.name: HTTP_CUSTOM_HEADER
params.comment: Add an extra custom HTTP header to the request. The first string is the name of the parameter to change, e.g. "Pragma", and the second string is the value, e.g. "no-cache". Multiple custom headers may be configured per request, as long as the combined custom header length is no greater than 4096 characters. Note that certain headers, such as the default headers, are blocked for security reasons.

params.name: HTTP_PRAGMA_NO_CACHE
params.comment: Sends "Pragma: no-cache" header (TRUE), or does not send a "Pragma" header (FALSE).

params.name: HTTP_USER_AGENT
params.comment: The user agent value is appended to the one generated by LSL itself. It should follow the syntax from the HTTP standard like: "My-Script-Name/1.0 (Mozilla compatible)". Note: Spaces are not allowed in HTTP User Agent token values, so "My Script Name/1.0" will produce a script error; change the spaces to hyphens ("-")

params.name: HTTP_ACCEPT
params.comment: HTTP_ACCEPT parameters can be passed to limit the number of mime types that are sent in the Accept: header of the HTTP request. Specified mime types may include character set and q parameters. This parameter may be specified multiple times. The specified mime type must be one already recognized by llHTTPRequest. These include any text/ mime type, or the following application mime types: “application/xhtml+xml”, “application/atom+xml”, “application/json”, “application/xml”, “application/llsd+xml”, “application/x-javascript”, “application/javascript”, “application/x-www-form-urlencoded”, or “application/rss+xml”. The Content-Type header in the response is checked against the specified HTTP_ACCEPT parameters. If the value of the header is not in the list of acceptable mime types, llHTTPRequest will return 415 as a result code and the body will be "Unsupported or unknown Content-Type."

params.name: HTTP_EXTENDED_ERROR
params.comment: If TRUE llHTTPRequest will always return a key. If there was an error making the HTTP request. Detailed error information will be returned through the http_response event using the provided key. Error information is delivered in a JSON block as described in RFC 7807. Details about extended return codes can be found below.

params.name: PSYS_PART_FLAGS
params.comment: Various flags controlling the behavior of the particle system. The value may be specified as an integer in decimal or hex format, or by ORing together (using the | operator) one or more of the following flag constants:

params.name: PSYS_SRC_PATTERN
params.comment: Specifies the general emission pattern.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_BURST_RADIUS
params.comment: Specifies the distance from the emitter where particles will be created. This rule is ignored when the PSYS_PART_FOLLOW_SRC_MASK flag is set. A test in http://forums-archive.secondlife.com/327/f5/226722/1.html indicates that the maximum value is 50.00

params.name: PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_BEGIN
params.comment: Specifies a half angle, in radians, of a circular or spherical "dimple" or conic section (starting from the emitter facing) within which particles will NOT be emitted. Valid values are the same as for PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_END, though the effects are reversed accordingly. If the pattern is PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE, the presentation is a 2D flat circular section. If PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE_CONE or PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE_CONE_EMPTY is used, the presentation is a 3D spherical section. Note that the value of this parameter and PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_END are internally re-ordered such that this parameter gets the smaller of the two values.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_END
params.comment: Specifies a half angle, in radians, of a circular or spherical "dimple" or conic section (starting from the emitter facing) within which particles WILL be emitted. Valid values are 0.0, which will result in particles being emitted in a straight line in the direction of the emitter facing, to PI, which will result in particles being emitted in a full circular or spherical arc around the emitter, not including the "dimple" or conic section defined by PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_BEGIN. If the pattern is PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE, the presentation is a 2D flat circular section. If PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE_CONE or PSYS_SRC_PATTERN_ANGLE_CONE_EMPTY is used, the presentation is a 3D spherical section. Note that the value of this parameter and PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_BEGIN are internally re-ordered such that this parameter gets the larger of the two values.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_INNERANGLE
params.comment: DEPRECATED: Use PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_BEGIN instead. Works similar to its replacement rule, except the edge of the section is aligned with the emitter facing, rather than its center.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_OUTERANGLE
params.comment: DEPRECATED: Use PSYS_SRC_ANGLE_END instead. Works similar to its replacement rule, except the edge of the section is aligned with the emitter facing, rather than the section's center.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_TARGET_KEY
params.comment: Specifies the key of a target object, prim, or agent towards which the particles will change course and move (if PSYS_PART_TARGET_POS_MASK is specified) or will move in a straight line (if PSYS_PART_TARGET_LINEAR_MASK is specified). They will attempt to end up at the geometric center of the target at the end of their lifetime. Requires the PSYS_PART_TARGET_POS_MASK or PSYS_PART_TARGET_LINEAR_MASK flag be set. caveat 4

params.name: PSYS_PART_START_COLOR
params.comment: A vector specifying the color of the particles upon emission.

params.name: PSYS_PART_END_COLOR
params.comment: A vector specifying the color the particles transition to during their lifetime. Only used if the PSYS_PART_INTERP_COLOR_MASK flag is set.

params.name: PSYS_PART_START_ALPHA
params.comment: Specifies the alpha of the particles upon emission. Valid values are in the range 0.0 to 1.0. Lower values are more transparent; higher ones are more opaque.

params.name: PSYS_PART_END_ALPHA
params.comment: Specifies the alpha the particles transition to during their lifetime. Only used if the PSYS_PART_INTERP_COLOR_MASK flag is set. Valid values are the same as PSYS_PART_START_ALPHA.

params.name: PSYS_PART_START_SCALE
params.comment: Specifies the scale or size of the particles upon emission. Valid values for each direction are 0.03125 to 4.0, in meters. The actual particle size is always a multiple of 0.03125. Smaller changes don't have any effect. Since particles are essentially 2D sprites, the Z component of the vector is ignored and can be set to 0.0.

params.name: PSYS_PART_END_SCALE
params.comment: Specifies the scale or size the particles transition to during their lifetime. Only used if the PSYS_PART_INTERP_SCALE_MASK flag is set. Valid values are the same as PSYS_PART_START_SCALE.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_TEXTURE
params.comment: Specifies the name of a texture in the emitter prim's inventory to use for each particle. Alternatively, you may specify an asset key UUID for a texture. If using llLinkParticleSystem and texture is not a UUID, texture must be in the emitter prim (not necessarily with the script).

params.name: PSYS_PART_START_GLOW
params.comment: Specifies the glow of the particles upon emission. Valid values are in the range of 0.0 (no glow) to 1.0 (full glow).

params.name: PSYS_PART_END_GLOW
params.comment: Specifies the glow that the particles transition to during their lifetime. Valid values are the same as PSYS_PART_START_GLOW.

params.name: PSYS_PART_BLEND_FUNC_SOURCE
params.comment: Specifies how blending function uses the incoming particle's color and alpha information to produce the rendered result. Defaults to PSYS_PART_BF_SOURCE_ALPHA.

params.name: PSYS_PART_BLEND_FUNC_DEST
params.comment: Specifies how blending function uses the current framebuffer's color and alpha information to produce the rendered result. Defaults to PSYS_PART_BF_ONE_MINUS_SOURCE_ALPHA. To make particles blend with the background in a less opaque and more luminescent way use PSYS_PART_BF_ONE for dest and the default for source. Most other blending combinations will render the invisible/alpha portion of your particle texture, unless the invisible area of your texture is all black (or, in some cases, unless it is all white).

params.name: PSYS_SRC_MAX_AGE
params.comment: Specifies the length of time, in seconds, that the emitter will operate upon coming into view range (if the particle system is already set) or upon execution of this function (if already in view range). Upon expiration, no more particles will be emitted, except as specified above. Zero will give the particle system an infinite duration. (caveat 1)

params.name: PSYS_PART_MAX_AGE
params.comment: Specifies the lifetime of each particle emitted, in seconds. Maximum is 30.0 seconds. During this time, the particle will appear, change appearance and move according to the parameters specified in the other sections, and then disappear.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_BURST_RATE
params.comment: Specifies the time interval, in seconds, between "bursts" of particles being emitted. Specifying a value of 0.0 will cause the emission of particles as fast as the viewer can do so.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_BURST_PART_COUNT
params.comment: Specifies the number of particles emitted in each "burst".

params.name: PSYS_SRC_ACCEL
params.comment: Specifies a directional acceleration vector applied to each particle as it is emitted, in meters per second squared. Valid values are 0.0 to 100.0 for each direction both positive and negative, as region coordinates.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_OMEGA
params.comment: Sets how far to rotate the "pattern" after each particle burst. (Burst frequency is set with PSYS_SRC_BURST_RATE.) Omega values are approximately 'radians per burst' around the prim's global (not local) X, Y, Z axes. For precise and predictable pattern rotation, rotate the prim instead of using PSYS_SRC_OMEGA. Omega has no visible effect on drop, explode and certain specific angle and angle cone patterns, depending on prim orientation. Pattern rotation can be used with prim orientation and llTargetOmega() but won't produce consistent results. (caveat 2 and caveat 3)

params.name: PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MIN
params.comment: Specifies the minimum value of a random range of values which is selected for each particle in a burst as its initial speed upon emission, in meters per second. Note that the value of this parameter and PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MAX are internally re-ordered such that this parameter gets the smaller of the two values.

params.name: PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MAX
params.comment: Specifies the maximum value of a random range of values which is selected for each particle in a burst as its initial speed upon emission, in meters per second. Note that the value of this parameter and PSYS_SRC_BURST_SPEED_MIN are internally re-ordered such that this parameter gets the larger of the two values.

params.name: CAMERA_ACTIVE
params.comment: Turns on or off scripted control of the camera.

params.name: CAMERA_BEHINDNESS_ANGLE
params.comment: Sets the angle in degrees within which the camera is not constrained by changes in target rotation.

params.name: CAMERA_BEHINDNESS_LAG
params.comment: Sets how strongly the camera is forced to stay behind the target if outside of behindness angle.

params.name: CAMERA_DISTANCE
params.comment: Sets how far away the camera wants to be from its target.

params.name: CAMERA_FOCUS
params.comment: Sets camera focus (target position) in region coordinates.

params.name: CAMERA_FOCUS_LAG
params.comment: How much the camera lags as it tries to aim towards the target.

params.name: CAMERA_FOCUS_LOCKED
params.comment: Locks the camera focus so it will not move.

params.name: CAMERA_FOCUS_OFFSET
params.comment: Adjusts the camera focus position relative to the target.

params.name: CAMERA_FOCUS_THRESHOLD
params.comment: Sets the radius of a sphere around the camera's target position within which its focus is not affected by target motion.

params.name: CAMERA_PITCH
params.comment: Adjusts the angular amount that the camera aims straight ahead vs. straight down, maintaining the same distance; analogous to 'incidence'."

params.name: CAMERA_POSITION
params.comment: Sets camera position in region coordinates.

params.name: CAMERA_POSITION_LAG
params.comment: How much the camera lags as it tries to move towards its 'ideal' position.

params.name: CAMERA_POSITION_LOCKED
params.comment: Locks the camera position so it will not move.

params.name: CAMERA_POSITION_THRESHOLD
params.comment: Sets the radius of a sphere around the camera's ideal position within which it is not affected by target motion.

params.name: RC_REJECT_TYPES
params.comment: Mask used to ignore specific types of objects (and avatars).

params.name: RC_DATA_FLAGS
params.comment: Described in the RC_DATA_FLAGS section.

params.name: RC_MAX_HITS
params.comment: Maximum number of hits to return. Maximum value is 256. To avoid performance issues, keep it small.

params.name: RC_DETECT_PHANTOM
params.comment: Set to TRUE (or nonzero) to detect phantom AND volume detect objects. It is not possible to detect only phantom objects or only volume detect objects. If set to TRUE, phantom and volume detect objects will always be detected, even if RC_REJECT_NONPHYSICAL and RC_REJECT_PHYSICAL are set in RC_REJECT_TYPES.

params.name: CHARACTER_DESIRED_SPEED
params.comment: Speed of pursuit in meters per second.

params.name: CHARACTER_RADIUS
params.comment: Set collision capsule radius.

params.name: CHARACTER_LENGTH
params.comment: Set collision capsule length If the value is less than twice the radius plus 0.1m, it will be set to twice the radius plus 0.1m.

params.name: CHARACTER_ORIENTATION
params.comment: Set the character orientation.

params.name: CHARACTER_TYPE
params.comment: Specifies which walkability coefficient will be used by this character.

params.name: CHARACTER_AVOIDANCE_MODE
params.comment: Allows you to specify that a character should not try to avoid other characters, should not try to avoid dynamic obstacles (relatively fast moving objects and avatars), or both. This is framed in the positive sense ([CHARACTER_AVOIDANCE_MODE, AVOID_CHARACTERS] would create a character that avoided other characters but not agents or moving vehicles). Setting this parameter to AVOID_NONE causes the character to not avoid either category.

params.name: CHARACTER_MAX_ACCEL
params.comment: The character's maximum acceleration rate.

params.name: CHARACTER_MAX_DECEL
params.comment: The character's maximum deceleration rate.

params.name: CHARACTER_DESIRED_TURN_SPEED
params.comment: The character's maximum speed while turning--note that this is only loosely enforced (i.e., a character may turn at higher speeds under certain conditions)

params.name: CHARACTER_MAX_TURN_RADIUS
params.comment: The character's turn radius when traveling at CHARACTER_DESIRED_TURN_SPEED

params.name: CHARACTER_MAX_SPEED
params.comment: The character's maximum speed. Affects speed when avoiding dynamic obstacles and when traversing low-walkability objects in TRAVERSAL_TYPE_FAST mode.

params.name: CHARACTER_ACCOUNT_FOR_SKIPPED_FRAMES
params.comment: TRUE matches pre-existing behavior. If set to FALSE, character will not attempt to catch up on lost time when pathfinding performance is low, potentially providing more reliable movement (albeit while potentially appearing to be more stuttery).

params.name: CHARACTER_STAY_WITHIN_PARCEL
params.comment: FALSE matches traditional behavior. If set to TRUE, treat the parcel boundaries as one-way obstacles (will re-enter but can't leave on it's own).

params.name: REZ_PARAM
params.comment: Start parameter passed into the rezzed object's on_rez(integer) event.

params.name: REZ_FLAGS
params.comment: Flags applied to rezzed object when it is created in the world. Flags parameter integer value description REZ_FLAG_TEMP 0x0001 Object is rezzed as temporary. REZ_FLAG_PHYSICAL 0x0002 Object is rezzed as physical. REZ_FLAG_PHANTOM 0x0004 Object is rezzed as phantom REZ_FLAG_DIE_ON_COLLIDE 0x0008 The object will die after its first collision. REZ_FLAG_DIE_ON_NOENTRY 0x0010 Object will die if it attempts to enter a parcel that it can't. REZ_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE_OWNER 0x0020 Object will not trigger a collision event if colliding with its owner.† REZ_FLAG_NO_COLLIDE_FAMILY 0x0040 Object will not trigger collision events when colliding with other object rezzed by the same rezzer.† REZ_FLAG_BLOCK_GRAB_OBJECT 0x0080 Grabbing is disabled for this object. † Disabling collisions only disables collision events and damage. The object will still cause a physics collision and may push the objects.

params.name: REZ_POS
params.comment: Position to rez the new object in the world. If relative is FALSE the position is in region coordinates. If relative is TRUE, the position will be relative to the rezzing object. If at_root is FALSE, the center of the object will be at the position specified by pos(llRezObject). Set at_root to TRUE to set the position of the root prim(llRezAtRoot).

params.name: REZ_ROT
params.comment: The initial rotation to apply to the object. If relative is TRUE, the rotation is relative to the rezzing object, otherwise it is absolute.

params.name: REZ_VEL
params.comment: The initial velocity to apply to the object. If local is TRUE the velocity is in the local object coordinate frame, otherwise it is in world coordinates. If inherit is TRUE the object also inherits it's rezzer's velocity.

params.name: REZ_ACCEL
params.comment: A constant force to apply to the object. If local is TRUE, the force vector is in local coordinates.

params.name: REZ_OMEGA
params.comment: Spin the object around the specified axis. If local is TRUE that axis is in local coordinates, otherwise they are global.

params.name: REZ_DAMAGE
params.comment: The amount of damage applied to an agent upon collision with this object.

params.name: REZ_SOUND
params.comment: A sound to attach to this object. It will be played at the specified volume. If loop is TRUE the sound will loop continuously for the life of the object. The sound parameter may be either a sound file in the rezzer's inventory or the UUID of a sound asset.

params.name: REZ_SOUND_COLLIDE
params.comment: A sound to play upon collision with another object, the ground or an avatar. The sound parameter may be either a sound file in the rezzer's inventory or the UUID of a sound asset.

params.name: REZ_LOCK_AXES
params.comment: Prevent the object from spinning on certain axes. Setting the vector's coordinate to non-zero will prevent the object from spinning on that axis. For instance REZ_LOCK_AXES, <1.0, 1.0, 0.0> will allow the object to only rotate around its Z-axis.

params.name: REZ_DAMAGE_TYPE
params.comment: The damage type to apply when this prim collides with another object. Can match one of the DAMAGE_TYPE_* constants, be a custom damage type or repurpose the damage field.

params.name: REZ_PARAM_STRING
params.comment: Pass an initialization string to the root prim of the newly rezzed object that may be read with llGetStartString from within the rezzed object. Maximum string length is 1024 bytes.

params.name: OBJECT_NAME
params.comment: Gets the prim's name. If id is an avatar, the Legacy Name is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_DESC
params.comment: Gets the prim's description. If id is an avatar, an empty string is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_POS
params.comment: Gets the prim's position in region coordinates. If id is an avatar outside the region (see above), this position is relative to the region the script is running in.

params.name: OBJECT_ROT
params.comment: Gets the prim's rotation.

params.name: OBJECT_VELOCITY
params.comment: Gets the object's velocity.

params.name: OBJECT_OWNER
params.comment: Gets an object's owner key. If id is an avatar, that avatar's key is returned (which is the same as id). If id is group-owned, a NULL_KEY is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_GROUP
params.comment: Gets the prim's group key. If id is an avatar, a NULL_KEY is returned, which means a workaround is required to get an avatar's active group.[1]

params.name: OBJECT_CREATOR
params.comment: Gets the prim's creator key. If id is an avatar, a NULL_KEY is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_RUNNING_SCRIPT_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the number of running scripts attached to the object or agent.

params.name: OBJECT_TOTAL_SCRIPT_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the number of scripts, both running and stopped, attached to the object or agent.

params.name: OBJECT_SCRIPT_MEMORY
params.comment: Gets the amount of script memory used by the object or agent, in bytes, or its upper limit. See page for more info.

params.name: OBJECT_SCRIPT_TIME
params.comment: Gets the total amount of average script CPU time used by the object or agent, in seconds. See page for more info.

params.name: OBJECT_PRIM_EQUIVALENCE
params.comment: Gets the prim equivalence of the object.

params.name: OBJECT_SERVER_COST
params.comment: Gets the server cost of the object.

params.name: OBJECT_STREAMING_COST
params.comment: Gets the streaming (download) cost of the object.

params.name: OBJECT_PHYSICS_COST
params.comment: Gets the physics cost of the object.

params.name: OBJECT_CHARACTER_TIME
params.comment: Gets the average CPU time (in seconds) used by the object for navigation, if the object is a pathfinding character. Returns 0 for non-characters.

params.name: OBJECT_ROOT
params.comment: Gets the id of the root prim of the object requested. If id is an avatar, returns the id of the root prim of the linkset the avatar is sitting on and linked to (or the avatar's own id if the avatar is not sitting on an object within the region).

params.name: OBJECT_ATTACHED_POINT
params.comment: Gets the attachment point to which the object is attached. It returns an integer matching one of the ATTACH_* constants.

params.name: OBJECT_PATHFINDING_TYPE
params.comment: Gets the pathfinding setting of the object in the region. It returns an integer matching one of the OPT_* constants.

params.name: OBJECT_PHYSICS
params.comment: Gets the integer boolean detailing if physics is enabled or disabled on the object. If id is an avatar or attachment, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_PHANTOM
params.comment: Gets the integer boolean detailing if phantom is enabled or disabled on the object. If id is an avatar or attachment, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_TEMP_ON_REZ
params.comment: Gets the integer boolean detailing if temporary is enabled or disabled on the object.

params.name: OBJECT_RENDER_WEIGHT
params.comment: Gets the avatar's render weight. If id is an object, 0 is returned. If id is an avatar whose render weight is unknown to the simulator, -1 is returned. The maximum render weight reported by the server is 500000[1].

params.name: OBJECT_HOVER_HEIGHT
params.comment: Gets the hover height of the avatar. If id is not an avatar, 0.0 is returned. Normal values are in the range [-2.0, 2.0] with a default of 0.0. This value does not reflect the avatar shape's "Hover" slider, only the dynamic viewer setting.

params.name: OBJECT_BODY_SHAPE_TYPE
params.comment: Gets a float which describes the sex setting of the avatar's currently worn shape. If id is not an avatar, -1.0 is returned. Normal operational values are in the range [0.0, 1.0]. 0.0 is standard female setting, 1.0 is standard male setting. Intermediate values with visible differences are possible with manually crafted shapes.

params.name: OBJECT_LAST_OWNER_ID
params.comment: Gets the UUID of the object's previous owner, if known. For group-owned objects, this is the avatar that deeded the object. Returns NULL_KEY for avatars, or objects that were never transferred. A rezzed object taken back to inventory, then re-rezzed, will return its current owner key.

params.name: OBJECT_CLICK_ACTION
params.comment: Gets the click action of the prim. It returns an integer matching one of the CLICK_ACTION_* constants.

params.name: OBJECT_OMEGA
params.comment: Gets the object's rotational velocity (radians per second).

params.name: OBJECT_PRIM_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the object's prim count

params.name: OBJECT_TOTAL_INVENTORY_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the object's total number of inventory items.

params.name: OBJECT_REZZER_KEY
params.comment: Gets the key of the object that rezzed this object be it an object or an avatar.

params.name: OBJECT_GROUP_TAG
params.comment: Gets the avatar's group tag text. If id is not an avatar, an empty string is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_TEMP_ATTACHED
params.comment: Gets the integer boolean detailing if the object is temporarily attached.

params.name: OBJECT_ATTACHED_SLOTS_AVAILABLE
params.comment: Gets the avatar's available attachment slot count. If id is not an avatar, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_CREATION_TIME
params.comment: Gets the object's creation time. This time is established with raw material rezzing through the build menu and with mesh uploads. This time is NOT established with inventory rezzes, scripted rezzes, object modifying, copying or transferring. If id is an avatar, an empty string is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_SELECT_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the total number of agents selecting any links in the object. If id is an avatar, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_SIT_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the total number of agents sitting on any links in the object. If id is an avatar, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_ANIMATED_COUNT
params.comment: Gets the integer boolean detailing if the object's root is set to "Animated Mesh" or gets the total number of "Animated Mesh" attachments worn by an agent.

params.name: OBJECT_ANIMATED_SLOTS_AVAILABLE
params.comment: Gets the avatar's available "Animated Mesh" attachment slot count. If id is not an avatar, 0 is returned.

params.name: OBJECT_ACCOUNT_LEVEL
params.comment: Gets the account level of an avatar. If id is not an avatar, -1 is returned. 0 is Basic account level. 1 is Premium account level. 5 is Plus account level. 10 is Premium Plus account level.

params.name: OBJECT_MATERIAL
params.comment: Retrieves the physics material set on this object. It returns an integer matching one of the PRIM_MATERIAL_* constants.

params.name: OBJECT_MASS
params.comment: Gets the mass (in Kilograms) of this object's linkset.

params.name: OBJECT_TEXT
params.comment: Gets the floating text displayed above this object.

params.name: OBJECT_REZ_TIME
params.comment: Retrieves the time that this object was rezzed.

params.name: OBJECT_LINK_NUMBER
params.comment: Get this object's index in the linkset.

params.name: OBJECT_SCALE
params.comment: Get the size of this object.

params.name: OBJECT_TEXT_COLOR
params.comment: Gets the color of the floating text displayed above this object.

params.name: OBJECT_TEXT_ALPHA
params.comment: Gets the alpha value of the floating text displayed above this object.

params.name: OBJECT_HEALTH
params.comment: Retrieves the health of an avatar or prim.

params.name: OBJECT_DAMAGE
params.comment: Retrieves the amount of damage a prim inflicts on collision.

params.name: OBJECT_DAMAGE_TYPE
params.comment: Retrieves the type of damage a prim inflicts on collision. It returns an integer that can match one of the DAMAGE_TYPE_* constants, be a custom damage type or be repurposed by a combat system.

params.name: OBJECT_PERMS
params.comment: Retrieves the permissions for this object as 5 integers.

params.name: OBJECT_PERMS_COMBINED
params.comment: Retrieves the permissions for this object combined with all of its inventory items as 5 integers.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_NAME
params.comment: The name of the parcel.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_DESC
params.comment: The description of the parcel.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_OWNER
params.comment: The parcel owner's key.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_GROUP
params.comment: The parcel group's key.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_AREA
params.comment: The parcel's area, in sqm.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_ID
params.comment: The parcel's key.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_SEE_AVATARS
params.comment: The parcel's avatar visibility setting[2]

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_PRIM_CAPACITY
params.comment: The total prim capacity on this and all same-owner parcels, sim-wide. See llGetParcelMaxPrims for same-parcel-only and/or sim-wide reporting.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_PRIM_USED
params.comment: The total prim usage on this and all same-owner parcels, sim-wide. See llGetParcelPrimCount to get prim count by parcel owner, group, temp, etc. for same-parcel-only and/or sim-wide reporting.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_LANDING_POINT
params.comment: Landing point set for this parcel, if any.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_LANDING_LOOKAT
params.comment: Look at vector set for the landing point on this parcel, if any.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_TP_ROUTING
params.comment: Teleport routing for this parcel. 0 = TP_ROUTING_BLOCKED 1 = TP_ROUTING_LANDINGP 2 = TP_ROUTING_FREE Note that routing rules are only enforced if the landing point is set.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_FLAGS
params.comment: Parcel flags set for this parcel. See llGetParcelFlags for a listing of the flags and their meaning.

params.name: PARCEL_DETAILS_SCRIPT_DANGER
params.comment: Is the script in danger in the indicated parcel. See llScriptDanger for a discussion of script danger.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_COLOR_FACTOR
params.comment: Set the tinting color used for the base color. Color is specified in linear RGB. Use llsRGB2Linear to convert colors from Blinn-Phong to PBR.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_ALPHA
params.comment: Sets the alpha for the face(s). Only impacts the rendering when the alpha mode is set to PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_ALPHA_MODE
params.comment: Set the alpha mode on the face(s). Must be one of the valid blend modes GLTF Alpha Mode value description PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_OPAQUE 0 Ignore the alpha value and render the material as opaque. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_BLEND 1 Render the material with transparency determined by the alpha value. Blending is done in linear color space. As is the case for Blinn-Phong as well, this mode suffers from depth sorting and performance issues. Use alpha mask instead when possible. PRIM_GLTF_ALPHA_MODE_MASK 2 Render the material as fully opaque where the alpha value is greater than the alpha cutoff, and otherwise render the material as fully transparent.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_ALPHA_MASK
params.comment: Sets the alpha cutoff level on the face(s) when alpha mode is set to mask.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_BASE_DOUBLE_SIDED
params.comment: If set to TRUE then the texture on the face(s) will be rendered as double sided.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_METALLIC_FACTOR
params.comment: Adjusts the metallic factor on the specified face(s). Value should be between 0 and 1.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_ROUGHNESS_FACTOR
params.comment: Adjust the roughness factor on the specified face(s). Value should be between 0 and 1.

params.name: OVERRIDE_GLTF_EMISSIVE_FACTOR
params.comment: Set the tint used for the emissive texture on the face(s). Note that this is specified in linear RGB.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_ALT_IMAGE_ENABLE
params.comment: Gets the default image state (the image that the user sees before a piece of media is active) for the chosen face. The default image is specified by Second Life's server for that media type. Note: This flag is not currently implemented.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS
params.comment: Gets the style of controls. Can be either PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS_STANDARD or PRIM_MEDIA_CONTROLS_MINI.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_CURRENT_URL
params.comment: Gets the current url displayed on the chosen face. Changing this URL causes navigation. 1024 characters Max

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_HOME_URL
params.comment: Gets the home url for the chosen face. 1024 characters max

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_LOOP
params.comment: Gets whether auto-looping is enabled.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_PLAY
params.comment: Gets whether the media auto-plays when a Resident can view it.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_SCALE
params.comment: Gets whether auto-scaling is enabled. Auto-scaling forces the media to the full size of the texture.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_AUTO_ZOOM
params.comment: Gets whether clicking the media triggers auto-zoom and auto-focus on the media.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_FIRST_CLICK_INTERACT
params.comment: Gets whether the first click interaction is enabled. Note: This flag appears not to work.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_WIDTH_PIXELS
params.comment: Gets the width of the media in pixels.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_HEIGHT_PIXELS
params.comment: Gets the height of the media in pixels.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST_ENABLE
params.comment: Gets whether navigation is restricted to URLs in PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_WHITELIST
params.comment: Gets the whitelist as a string of escaped, comma-separated URLs. This string can hold up to 64 URLs or 1024 characters, whichever comes first.

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_PERMS_INTERACT
params.comment: Gets the permissions mask that control who can interact with the object: PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_NONE PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_OWNER PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_GROUP PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_ANYONE

params.name: PRIM_MEDIA_PERMS_CONTROL
params.comment: Gets the permissions mask that control who can see the media control bar above the object: PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_NONE PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_OWNER PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_GROUP PRIM_MEDIA_PERM_ANYONE

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_TEXTURE
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel's media texture.

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_URL
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel's media url.

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_TYPE
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel media MIME type (e.g. "text/html"). (1.19.1 RC0 or later)

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_SIZE
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel media pixel resolution. (1.19.1 RC0 or later)

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_DESC
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel media description. (1.19.1 RC0 or later)

params.name: PARCEL_MEDIA_COMMAND_LOOP_SET
params.comment: Used to get or set the parcel's media loop duration. (1.19.1 RC0 or later)

params.name: SKY_TRACKS
params.comment: Altitudes for sky transitions in the region.

params.name: SKY_AMBIENT
params.comment: The ambient color of the environment.

params.name: SKY_TEXTURE_DEFAULTS
params.comment: Checks if the textures are currently set to use the default. For default values, the returned integer is 1. If the texture uses something other than the default, the returned value is 0.

params.name: SKY_CLOUDS
params.comment: Environmental cloud information. color: The color used for the clouds. coverage: The coverage percentage. scale: The scaling applied to the cloud textures. variance: A randomizing factor applied to the main cloud layer scroll: The scroll speed of the clouds. X is east/west Y is north/south Z is unused density: The X/Y and D parameter used to generate cloud density detail: The X/Y and D parameter used to generate cloud details. is_default: 1 if the clouds are using the default texture.

params.name: SKY_DOME
params.comment: Sky dome information. offset radius maximum altitude

params.name: SKY_GAMMA
params.comment: The gamma value applied to the scene. In viewer versions 7.0+, this value has been repurposed into the "HDR Scale" value in the EEP editor. (Thus, this will return the value of the HDR Scale slider).

params.name: SKY_GLOW
params.comment: Glow applied to the sun and moon. size of glow effect focus of glow effect

params.name: SKY_MOON
params.comment: Detailed moon information rot: The current rotation applied to the moon. scale: The current scale applied to the moon's texture brightness: The moon's brightness is_default_texture: 1 if the moon texture is set to the default. 0 otherwise direction: A unit vector pointing at the moon. ambient_color: The ambient color of the moon diffuse_color: The diffuse color applied to the moon.

params.name: SKY_SUN
params.comment: Detailed sun information rot: The current rotation applied to the sun. scale: The current scale applied to the sun's texture sun_color: is_default_texture: 1 if the sun texture is set to the default. 0 otherwise direction: A unit vector pointing at the sun. ambient_color: The ambient color of the sun. diffuse_color: The diffuse color applied to the sun.

params.name: SKY_PLANET
params.comment: Planet information used in rendering the sky planet_radius sky_bottom_radius sky_top_radius

params.name: SKY_REFRACTION
params.comment: Sky refraction parameters for rainbows and optical effects. moisture_level droplet_radius ice_level

params.name: SKY_LIGHT
params.comment: Miscellaneous lighting values light_direction: A unit vector indicating the direction of the dominant light source. fade_color: A color vector representing the current color of the light emitted from the dominant light source (in sRGB space). total_ambient: A color vector representing the current ambient color in use in the scene (in sRGB space).

params.name: SKY_REFLECTION_PROBE_AMBIANCE
params.comment: Minimum ambiance value for all reflection probes. range = [0.0, 10.0] Caveat: This parameter will be supported in the upcoming GLTF Materials project. Currently it will only work in supported testing areas with a supported test viewer.

params.name: WATER_BLUR_MULTIPLIER
params.comment: Multiplier applied to blur the scene when under water.

params.name: WATER_FOG
params.comment: Fog parameters applied when underwater color: The color of the underwater fog density: Density exponent applied to the fog modulation:

params.name: WATER_FRESNEL
params.comment: Fresnel scattering applied to the surface of the water. offset scale

params.name: WATER_TEXTURE_DEFAULTS
params.comment: Checks if the textures are currently set to use the default. For default values the returned integer is 1, if the texture uses something other than the default this value is 0.

params.name: WATER_NORMAL_SCALE
params.comment: Scaling applied to the water normal map.

params.name: WATER_REFRACTION
params.comment: Refraction factors when looking through the surface of the water. scale_above scale_below

params.name: WATER_WAVE_DIRECTION
params.comment: Vector for the directions of the waves Y represents north/south and X represents movement east/west. large_wave: Large wave speed and direction. small_wave: Small wave speed and direction.

params.name: ENVIRONMENT_DAYINFO
params.comment: Current time and day information day_length: Number of seconds in the environments day cycle. day_offset: Number of seconds day cycle is offset from GMT. secs_since_midnight: Number of seconds elapsed since the last day cycle midnight.
